message:
refactor(raptorq): minimum-degree row selection for constraint matrix pivoting

Replace full pivoting (row + column search) with minimum-degree row
selection during forward elimination. At each step the algorithm now
picks the unprocessed row with the fewest nonzero entries in unused
columns, preferentially pivoting on identity rows (degree 1).

This is a simplified form of RFC 6330 Section 5.4's inactivation
decoding heuristic and prevents identity rows from losing their column
to denser rows during elimination. The approach tracks used_row[] and
computes degree on the fly, short-circuiting when degree == 1 is found.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
